questions.question: –ö–∞–∫–∏–µ —Ç—Ä–∏ –∫–æ–º–ø–æ–Ω–µ–Ω—Ç–∞ –Ω—É–∂–Ω—ã –¥–ª—è –æ–ø—Ä–µ–¥–µ–ª–µ–Ω–∏—è event-time table —Å watermark –≤ DDL?

options.text: (1) TIMESTAMP(3) –∏–ª–∏ TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) column –∏–∑ payload (–ª–∏–±–æ computed —á–µ—Ä–µ–∑ TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ –∏–∑ epoch); (2) WATERMARK FOR <column> AS <column> - INTERVAL 'n' SECOND ‚Äî defines max out-of-orderness tolerance; (3) WITH connector properties (kafka, format –∏ –¥—Ä). Computed columns –º–æ–≥—É—Ç –±—ã—Ç—å –∏—Å–ø–æ–ª—å–∑–æ–≤–∞–Ω—ã –≤ WATERMARK declaration

questions.explanation: Event-time DDL three pieces: **(1) Time column**: `event_time TIMESTAMP(3)` direct –∏–∑ JSON/Avro field, –∏–ª–∏ computed `event_time AS TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ(ts_millis, 3)` –¥–ª—è Unix epoch millis. TIMESTAMP_LTZ recommended –¥–ª—è multi-timezone ‚Äî represents instant in time, correct globally. **(2) Watermark declaration**: `WATERMARK FOR event_time AS event_time - INTERVAL '5' SECOND` ‚Äî Flink computes watermark –∫–∞–∫ max(event_time observed) - 5s. Records —Å event_time < watermark —Å—á–∏—Ç–∞—é—Ç—Å—è late (–º–æ–≥—É—Ç drop –∏–ª–∏ go to side output). Choose interval based on measured P99 out-of-orderness + 50% safety margin. **(3) WITH clause**: connector-specific properties ‚Äî `'connector' = 'kafka'`, topic, bootstrap servers, format, etc. –ë–µ–∑ —ç—Ç–æ–≥–æ DDL –Ω–µ valid. **Common patterns**: (a) Kafka epoch timestamp: `ts BIGINT` raw + `event_time AS TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ(ts, 3)` computed + watermark on computed. (b) Direct TIMESTAMP –≤ JSON ISO format: `'json.timestamp-format.standard' = 'ISO-8601'` + `event_time TIMESTAMP(3)`. (c) From Kafka metadata: `record_kafka_ts TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) METADATA FROM 'timestamp'` ‚Äî use Kafka broker timestamp –∫–∞–∫ event_time. **Critical**: watermark must be on TIMESTAMP(3) or TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) ‚Äî other types error at planning.

questions.question: –ú–µ—Ç–∞–¥–∞–Ω–Ω—ã–µ Kafka (timestamp, partition, offset, headers) –Ω—É–∂–Ω—ã –≤ Flink SQL. –ö–∞–∫ extract –∏—Ö –≤ DDL?

options.text: –ß–µ—Ä–µ–∑ METADATA columns: `record_kafka_ts TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) METADATA FROM 'timestamp'`, `record_partition INT METADATA FROM 'partition' VIRTUAL`, `record_offset BIGINT METADATA FROM 'offset' VIRTUAL`, `record_headers MAP<STRING, BYTES> METADATA FROM 'headers' VIRTUAL`. VIRTUAL = read-only (–Ω–µ emit –≤ downstream –ø—Ä–∏ INSERT INTO). Headers MAP<STRING, BYTES> ‚Äî –Ω—É–∂–µ–Ω CAST AS STRING –¥–ª—è readable values

questions.explanation: METADATA columns ‚Äî declarative way access connector-specific metadata. Syntax `column_name type METADATA FROM 'key' [VIRTUAL]`. **Kafka available metadata**: `timestamp` (TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) ‚Äî broker assigned), `partition` (INT), `offset` (BIGINT), `headers` (MAP<STRING, BYTES>), `topic` (STRING), `key` (depends on key format). **VIRTUAL keyword**: column is read-only, –Ω–µ included –∫–æ–≥–¥–∞ INSERT INTO this table –∏–ª–∏ downstream propagation. Useful –¥–ª—è metadata used –≤ query –Ω–æ –Ω–µ emit-ed –∫ sink. **Use cases**: (1) **Dedup by offset**: `SELECT ... FROM events WHERE record_offset = MAX(record_offset) OVER (PARTITION BY business_id)` ‚Äî deduplicate using Kafka offset. (2) **Use Kafka timestamp –∫–∞–∫ event time**: `event_time AS record_kafka_ts`, watermark on this. Cheap alternative –∫–æ–≥–¥–∞ payload –Ω–µ –∏–º–µ–µ—Ç timestamp. (3) **Header-based routing**: `WHERE CAST(record_headers['region'] AS STRING) = 'us-east-1'` ‚Äî filter by Kafka header. (4) **Partition-aware processing**: `PARTITION BY record_partition` ‚Äî per-partition aggregations. **Format note**: Headers MAP<STRING, BYTES> requires CAST to STRING –¥–ª—è readable values: `CAST(record_headers['key'] AS STRING)`. **Other connectors**: each connector exposes own metadata. FileSystem: `file.path`, `file.name`, `partition`. Iceberg: `data_file_path`, `snapshot_id`. CDC: `op` (I/U/D), `before` row, `after` row.

questions.question: Computed columns: –∫–∞–∫–∏–µ limitation –∏ use cases?

options.text: Computed columns (`col_name AS expression`): (a) not stored (calculated at runtime); (b) can use built-in functions, UDFs; (c) can only reference previously-declared columns (ordering matters); (d) can be used in WATERMARK declaration. Use cases: TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ conversion, derived fields (total = amount + tax), string manipulation, PROCTIME(), JSON path extraction

questions.explanation: Computed columns ‚Äî declarative derived fields. **Syntax**: `column_name AS expression` –≤ CREATE TABLE column list. **Properties**: (1) **Not stored** ‚Äî computed at runtime when row accessed. Like Postgres GENERATED ALWAYS AS but virtual. (2) **Reference earlier columns only**: `c AS d + 1` if d –Ω–µ declared yet ‚Äî planning error. Order columns carefully. (3) **Use built-ins + UDFs**: arithmetic, string functions, time functions, custom UDFs registered. (4) **Used in WATERMARK**: `WATERMARK FOR event_time AS event_time - INTERVAL '5' SECOND` where event_time is computed column. **Common patterns**: (a) **Time conversion**: `event_time AS TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ(ts_millis, 3)` –¥–ª—è epoch ‚Üí TIMESTAMP_LTZ. Then watermark on event_time. (b) **Total —Å derived value**: `total AS amount + tax` ‚Äî readable, –Ω–µ store separately. (c) **String manipulation**: `region AS SUBSTRING(user_id, 1, 2)` ‚Äî extract prefix. (d) **Boolean flags**: `is_large AS amount > 1000`. (e) **PROCTIME()**: `proc_time AS PROCTIME()` ‚Äî processing time attribute. (f) **JSON extraction**: `details AS JSON_VALUE(payload, '$.metadata.region')`. **Difference vs metadata columns**: computed = expression-based –æ—Ç –¥—Ä—É–≥–∏—Ö columns. Metadata = from connector layer. Both not stored but different sources. **Performance**: computed columns add CPU work ‚Äî keep simple. Complex transformations better –≤ downstream processing (FROM source SELECT ...).

questions.question: `PRIMARY KEY (col) NOT ENFORCED` –≤ Flink SQL ‚Äî —ç—Ç–æ constraint –∫–æ—Ç–æ—Ä—ã–π Flink –≤–∞–ª–∏–¥–∏—Ä—É–µ—Ç –≤–æ –≤—Ä–µ–º—è writes (–∫–∞–∫ –≤ RDBMS).
questions.explanation: False. **NOT ENFORCED keyword** explicit ‚Äî Flink does NOT validate uniqueness. Flink ‚Äî —ç—Ç–æ streaming engine, –Ω–µ database. –ù–µ –∏–º–µ–µ—Ç capability –ø—Ä–æ–≤–µ—Ä–∏—Ç—å uniqueness –±–µ–∑ full table scan (O(N) per row insert). **What PRIMARY KEY does**: (1) **Metadata signal** –¥–ª—è query optimizer ‚Äî informs cardinality estimates, –º–æ–∂–µ—Ç switch operator implementations. (2) **Required –¥–ª—è upsert-kafka connector** ‚Äî defines compact topic key. (3) **Required –¥–ª—è temporal join target table** ‚Äî versioned table needs PK –¥–ª—è identity. (4) **Required –¥–ª—è some sinks** ‚Äî JDBC upsert (key –¥–ª—è ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE), Iceberg equality deletes, Paimon UPSERT. (5) **Influences partitioning at sink** ‚Äî records hashed by PK columns. **User responsibility**: ensure data actually has unique PK values. If duplicates exist –≤ source: (a) upsert sinks treat as updates (–ø–æ—Å–ª–µ–¥–Ω–∏–π wins) ‚Äî –º–æ–∂–µ—Ç lose data; (b) joins may produce unexpected duplicates; (c) materialized aggregations show wrong counts. **Composite PK syntax**: `PRIMARY KEY (region, user_id) NOT ENFORCED` ‚Äî multi-column. Order matters –¥–ª—è sharding –ø–æ hash composite key. **ENFORCED option exists conceptually –Ω–æ not supported** ‚Äî Flink would need to maintain unique index, costly. Future Flink versions might add –¥–ª—è small dim tables. **Always use NOT ENFORCED currently**.
